notes: Short Gulf crossing from Bushehr to Bahrain. 5th Fleet HQ partially hit. Video showed Shahed striking tower near naval headquarters.
Short-range strike on Erbil airport. Hosts US and coalition forces. One drone intercepted.

Primary drone corridor to Israel: Kermanshah across Iraq, Syria, Jordan to Tel Aviv. ~1,300km. Drones overfly 3 countries. IAF intercepted 100+ drones on this axis. NATO assets in Eastern Mediterranean also engaged.

sources.name: Estimated from launch regions and reported overfly path

notes: Northern Israel corridor: Isfahan across Iraq and Syria to Haifa. Longer route. Israeli defence systems intercepted missiles over Haifa per Euronews footage.

sources.name: Estimated from Euronews footage of Haifa intercepts

notes: Interpolated route: Kermanshah → Iraq airspace → Jordan airspace → Muwaffaq al-Salti Air Base (Azraq)
Interpolated route: Kermanshah → Iraq airspace → Jordan airspace → US THAAD Radar Site
Short-range corridor from Khuzestan across Iraq border region to Ali al-Salem. Shortest route in the campaign — only ~250km. All ballistic missiles intercepted.

sources.name: Estimated from launch and target coordinates

notes: Drone reached Kuwait International Airport. Minor injuries to employees, material damage to passenger building.
Longest drone route — Chabahar to Duqm along Omani coast. Both drones hit port. Oman had no Shahed-class air defence. 0% interception.
Direct Gulf crossing to Al Udeid (CENTCOM forward HQ). All intercepted by Patriot batteries.

sources.name: Estimated from launch and target coordinates

notes: Primary strike corridor: Isfahan launch area across southern Iran, through Strait of Hormuz to Al Dhafra. Direct route — no evasion needed as drones fly over Iranian territory until the Gulf crossing.

sources.name: Estimated from launch region and target coordinates

notes: Drone corridor to Dubai International Airport. Crossed Gulf on direct bearing. DXB Terminal 3 hit — evacuated, flights suspended.

sources.name: Estimated from Euronews / CNN reporting on DXB strike

notes: Drone struck near Fairmont hotel on Palm Jumeirah. 4 injured from debris and blast. Fire broke out.

sources.name: Estimated from TIME / Al Jazeera reporting

notes: Debris from aerial interception caused fire at Jebel Ali Port — one of world's largest container ports.
BM to Beit Shemesh — INTERCEPTORS FAILED. ~500kg warhead destroyed synagogue and bomb shelter. 9 killed, 49 injured. Deadliest single strike on Israel. IAF investigating intercept failure.
BM struck Tel Aviv residential block. 1 killed (home health aide), dozens wounded.
Interpolated route: Southern Lebanon → Northern Israel
Interpolated route: Khuzestan → Persian Gulf (north) → Saudi airspace → US Embassy Riyadh
Interpolated route: Khuzestan → Persian Gulf (north) → Saudi airspace → Ras Tanura Refinery
Interpolated route: Bushehr → Persian Gulf (north) → Bapco Refinery
Interpolated route: Kermanshah → Iraq airspace → Syria airspace → Tel Aviv
Interpolated route: Kermanshah → Iraq airspace → Syria airspace → Nevatim Air Base
Interpolated route: Bushehr → Persian Gulf (mid) → Mesaieed LNG
Interpolated route: Bushehr → Persian Gulf (mid) → Ras Laffan LNG
Sea-launched drone attack from IRGC Navy vessel. First known ship-based OWA drone attack in conflict. Short range — vessels positioned mid-Gulf for quicker strike time.

sources.name: Estimated from Navy Times / CENTCOM reporting

notes: Interpolated route: Kermanshah → Iraq airspace → Jordan airspace → Aqaba
Interpolated route: Bushehr → Persian Gulf (mid) → Qatar
Long-range drone corridor to Shaybah — deep in Saudi desert near UAE border. All 6 drones intercepted. Targeting energy infrastructure.
Interpolated route: Isfahan → Shiraz → Bandar Abbas → Strait of Hormuz → Al Dhafra Air Base
Interpolated route: Khuzestan → Persian Gulf (north) → Kuwait City high-rise
Interpolated route: Khuzestan → Persian Gulf (north) → Kuwait International Airport
FIRST NATO INTERCEPTION of the conflict. Iranian missile directed toward Turkey intercepted by NATO air defense systems.
Interpolated route: Isfahan → Shiraz → Bandar Abbas → Strait of Hormuz → Al Dhafra Air Base
Interpolated route: Isfahan → Shiraz → Bandar Abbas → Strait of Hormuz → Al Dhafra Satcom Facilities
Ballistic missile with cluster sub-munition warhead. Launched after Mojtaba Khamenei named new Supreme Leader.
Long-range drone route to Shaybah in the Empty Quarter. Intercepted by Saudi air defenses.
Struck residential facility in Al-Kharj — 2 Saudi civilians killed, first Saudi civilian deaths of the conflict.
Direct route from Hajiabad base across Gulf of Oman to Fujairah. Debris from interception caused fire at oil storage tanks.
Drone struck residential building in Al Seef, Manama. 1 woman killed, 8 injured.

sources.name: Estimated from launch region and target

notes: Strike route to Ruwais/ADNOC refinery. Drone hit industrial complex causing fire, forcing shutdown of 922,000 bpd refinery.

sources.name: Estimated from launch region and target

notes: Drone struck Bahrain airport on Muharraq Island, setting fuel tanks on fire.

sources.name: Estimated from launch region and target

notes: Strike on coalition military camp near Erbil. US soldiers wounded.

sources.name: Estimated from launch region and target

notes: Largest single drone wave against Saudi Arabia — 50 drones within hours. Most intercepted by Patriot systems.

sources.name: Estimated from launch region and target

notes: Shahed drone hit fuel storage tank at Dubai Int'l Airport. Flights suspended 7+ hours. World's busiest airport by international passengers.

sources.name: Estimated from launch region and target

notes: Drone struck Shah gas field operated by ADNOC/Occidental, sparking fire.

sources.name: Estimated from launch region and target

notes: Ballistic missile trajectory. Debris fell on Temple Mount (Al-Aqsa damage), Church of Holy Sepulchre, Jewish Quarter. Beit Shemesh synagogue shelter struck — 9 killed.

sources.name: Estimated from launch region and target

notes: Strike on Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG facility — world's largest LNG complex. 'Extensive damage' cutting ~17% of output potentially for 5 years.

sources.name: Estimated from launch region and target

notes: Ballistic missile from Tabriz to Haifa. Bazan oil refinery damaged. 5 salvos at Jerusalem/northern Israel within 1 hour.

sources.name: Estimated from launch region and target

notes: Drone struck Kuwait's largest oil refinery (730,000 bpd). Fires at multiple units. Attack during Eid al-Fitr.

sources.name: Estimated from launch region and target

notes: Drones targeted Ras Tanura oil terminal. Most intercepted by Saudi Patriot systems.

sources.name: Estimated from launch region and target

notes: IRBM trajectory to Diego Garcia — ~4,000km range. Both missiles missed (1 failed mid-flight, 1 possibly intercepted by SM-3). First use of IRBMs in the war, revealing Iran has greater range than publicly admitted. Unprecedented targeting of Indian Ocean base.

Drone took a highly circuitous route — north through central Ukraine past Kyiv, then west. Classic evasion pattern to confuse directional air defences. Source: Shahed Tracker tweet showing approximate movement through oblasts.

Drone looped — went west through Vinnytsia and Khmelnytskyi, continued to Ternopil, then returned to Khmelnytskyi. Looping behaviour observed increasingly in late 2025 as drones attempt to exhaust interceptor coverage in a region.
Two UAVs reached Starokostyantyniv from the west. Notable because Starokostyantyniv hosts a Ukrainian air base — likely deliberate targeting of military aviation infrastructure.
Explicit route from @ShahedTracker: looped north through Kyiv then west to Khmelnytskyi
Explicit route from @ShahedTracker: looping pattern — went to Ternopil then doubled back to Khmelnytskyi
Explicit route from @ShahedTracker: long circuitous path from south coast to western Ukraine

From @ShahedTracker: hit critical infrastructure, fire at substation in Sumy
